sharedEntities.description: Core entity representing a financial loan with properties such as ID, number, status, amount, and interest rate that flows between all components of the system
Represents banking accounts used for transfers and financial operations in the loan selling process
Represents financial transfers between accounts with status tracking for loan operations
Defines relationship between MPL and bank with terms for loan operations
Groups multiple loans for bulk processing operations across the system
Tracks interest accruals and calculations for loans over time

dataFlows.name: Loan Purchase Flow
dataFlows.description: Process of marketplace lenders purchasing loans through the platform
steps: Contract terms established between MPL and bank
Loans grouped into batches for purchase
Purchase invoice generated with loan details
Transfer of funds initiated between accounts

dataFlows.name: Interest Accrual Flow
dataFlows.description: Daily process of calculating and applying interest to loans
steps: Daily interest calculations triggered via outbox pattern
Interest rates (potentially including SOFR rates) applied to loan balances
Interest history records created or updated
Accounting service notified of interest accruals

dataFlows.name: Fee Management Flow
dataFlows.description: Process for calculating, collecting and distributing various fees
steps: Regular fee calculations performed based on loan activity
Volume-based fees calculated with monthly minimums applied
True-up adjustments made to ensure fee compliance
Accounting system updated with fee information

integrationPoints.description: Web API uses DbModel as its persistence layer, storing and retrieving loan, account, transfer and other entity data
Web API depends on Contracts to define data structures for loan selling operations and external service communications
Multiple outbox patterns ensure reliable message delivery to external systems for transfers, notifications, batch operations, fees, and interest calculations
Database tables implement outbox pattern storing events for asynchronous processing by external systems

patterns.description: Used extensively for reliable message delivery and event publishing to ensure consistency between the database and downstream systems
Used to abstract database operations and provide clean domain model interactions
Shared contracts repository defines data structures used across services to ensure consistency
System uses events via outboxes to coordinate processes across services and ensure reliable background processing

ambiguities: In COS.Lending.Selling.WebApi, the exact triggers for interest accrual calculations (whether time-based, event-based, or manual) are not clearly defined
In Cos.Lending.Selling.DbModel, the relationship between LoanAccount and Loan entities and when each is used is ambiguous
In Cos.Lending.Selling.Contracts, the exact relationship between Invoice and Receipt entities and how they map to the purchase flow is unclear
